title: Adana Kebab

seo.title: How to Make Adana Kebab — Easy Turkish Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Adana Kebab. This easy Turkish recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Adana Kebab — a beloved Turkish classic

description: Adana Kebab is a fiery and flavorful minced meat kebab, traditionally made from hand-chopped lamb or beef and tail fat, seasoned with red pepper flakes and sumac. This iconic dish from Adana, Turkey, is grilled on wide skewers, resulting in a juicy interior and a perfectly charred exterior. It is often served with lavash bread, fresh salads, and a dollop of yogurt, offering a truly authentic taste of Turkish cuisine.

ingredients.notes: or a mix of lamb and beef, 80/20 fat ratio

instructions.title: Prepare the Meat Mixture
instructions.text: Combine ground lamb, finely chopped red and green bell peppers, grated onion (squeezed dry), minced garlic, red pepper flakes, sweet paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.

instructions.title: Knead the Mixture Thoroughly
instructions.text: Using your hands, knead the meat mixture for at least 10-15 minutes until it becomes very sticky and cohesive. This helps the kebab stick to the skewers and achieves the characteristic Adana texture.

instructions.title: Chill the Meat
instructions.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the meat mixture for at least 30 minutes, or preferably an hour. This helps the flavors meld and the mixture firm up, making it easier to handle.

instructions.title: Form the Kebabs
instructions.text: Divide the chilled meat mixture into 4-6 equal portions. Take one portion and firmly press it around a wide, flat metal skewer, shaping it into an elongated, even layer about 1 inch thick. Ensure it adheres well to the skewer.

instructions.title: Grill the Kebabs
instructions.text: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Place the skewers directly over the heat and grill for 4-6 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until nicely charred and cooked through. The exact time depends on your grill and desired doneness.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Carefully slide the cooked kebabs off the skewers onto serving plates. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired and serve hot with warm lavash bread, sliced onions with sumac, grilled tomatoes, and green peppers for an authentic experience.

tips: For authentic texture, use good quality ground lamb with at least 20-25% fat content. If using leaner meat, add some finely chopped lamb tail fat or beef fat.
The kneading process is crucial for Adana Kebab; it develops the protein structure, making the meat stick to the skewer and preventing it from falling off during grilling.
Serve Adana Kebab immediately after grilling with warm lavash, a simple onion and sumac salad, and grilled vegetables like tomatoes and green peppers for a complete experience.
article: Adana Kebab is a beloved Turkish Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
